summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 't-distribution'?

"t-distribution" means: A symmetric, bell-shaped distribution with heavier tails than the normal, parameterized by degrees of freedom (df = n-1 for one sample). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Degrees of freedom (df)'?

"Degrees of freedom (df)" means: df = n - 1 for a single sample; controls the shape of the t-distribution—more df means closer to normal. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 't-statistic'?

"t-statistic" means: t = (x̄ - μ₀)/(s/√n); replaces the z-statistic when σ is unknown, using sample standard deviation s in the denominator. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Heavy tails'?

"Heavy tails" means: t-distributions have more probability in the tails than the normal, producing wider confidence intervals and larger critical values for small samples. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Robustness'?

"Robustness" means: t-procedures are robust to moderate non-normality for large samples but can be misleading for small samples from highly skewed distributions. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

Which term is described by: "t-procedures are robust to moderate non-normality for large samples but can be misleading for small samples from highly skewed distributions"

This description refers to "Robustness".
